Tests if dividing `undefined` by `1` results in `NaN` using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks if dividing `undefined` by `1` results in `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression `isNaN(undefined / 1) === true` evaluates to `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles arithmetic operations involving `undefined`, which should result in `NaN`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the JavaScript language.
